baseline_note: Every visual benchmark expects tool availability to be checked before capture. Never assumes screenshots-ios is installed — always verifies and falls back to xcrun simctl if unavailable. Never requires a fresh build for quick checks.

evals.prompt: Take a quick screenshot of my running iOS app to check if the home screen looks correct
evals.expected_output: Screenshot captured using the available tool, analyzed for visual issues, findings reported
expectations: Checks which capture tool is available before attempting capture
Falls back to xcrun simctl if screenshots-ios is unavailable
Does not require a fresh build for a quick check of an already-running simulator
Reports at least one specific visual observation about the captured screen

evals.prompt: Run visual regression on all my app screens and tell me what changed since the last approved baseline
evals.expected_output: Current screenshots compared against screenshots/baseline/, each screen classified as Pass, Expected change, or Regression
expectations: Reads both current and baseline images with the Read tool
Classifies each screen as Pass, Expected change, or Regression
Provides specific descriptions of what changed for any Regression finding

evals.prompt: Compare my Expenses list screen against the Figma export I'm providing and tell me exactly what's different
evals.expected_output: Element-level comparison between captured screenshot and design reference, findings prioritized as P1/P2
expectations: Reads both the captured screenshot and the provided design reference with the Read tool
Reports element-level differences (position, size, color, typography)
Classifies each finding as P1 (visible to users, must fix) or P2 (subtle, optional)
For each P1 finding, identifies the likely SwiftUI modifier or token to change
